provenance.pin_detail: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

privilege_notes: runs fetched package code at launch

findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'socraticode' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: socraticode.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'socraticode' package spec 'socraticode' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

evidence.reason: no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now)

findings.title: Server requires sensitive credentials
findings.detail: Server 'socraticode' requires credential-like env keys: GOOGLE_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, QDRANT_API_KEY.
findings.recommendation: Confirm each credential is scoped to least privilege and rotatable. Note which tools can exfiltrate it.

tools.name: codebase_graph_visualize
tools.description:
Visualise the code dependency graph. Two modes:
  • mode="mermaid" (default) — returns a Mermaid diagram (text) colour-coded by language, circular deps highlighted. Best for inline rendering inside chat, GitHub, or editors that render Mermaid.
  • mode="interactive" — writes a self-contained HTML page (vendored Cytoscape.js + Dagre, works offline) and opens it in the user's default browser. Shows the file graph and, when a symbol graph is available and fits, a Symbols toggle with the symbol-level call graph. Interactions: click node for sidebar with imports/dependents/symbols list; right-click node to highlight its blast radius (reverse-transitive closure); live search; layout switcher (Dagre / force / concentric / breadth-first / grid / circle); PNG export. Use this when the user asks for a visual/interactive view, wants to explore visually, or needs a shareable diagram.

data_categories.rationale: Full text of private communications — the actual content, not just who talked to whom.
data_categories.validation_reason: 'chat' is mentioned only as an output destination format, not as content the tool handles or accesses.

tools.name: codebase_impact
tools.description: Impact Analysis — return the BLAST RADIUS for a file or symbol. Lists every file (and, where helpful, function) that could break if you change the target. Polymorphic on target: a path-like string ('src/foo.ts') triggers file-mode; a name-like string ('validateUser') triggers symbol-mode. Use this BEFORE refactoring, renaming, or deleting code to know what depends on it.

tools.name: codebase_flow
tools.description: Trace the EXECUTION FLOW forward from an entry point — what does this code call into?
